title: lecanopteris pustulata subsp. howensis
intro: Lecanopteris pustulata subsp. howensis, commonly known as the Howea fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. It can be prepared as a tea or infused oil for topical application, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing and reduced inflammation.
